_doc: Project conventions checked by .claude/scripts/check-conventions.mjs. Each rule scans changed files (staged + unstaged + last commit) for forbiddenPattern within filesGlob minus excludeGlob, and exits 1 if any match. Edit the rules below to fit your project — add new ones, remove ones that don't apply. The script ships with no rules of its own; this file is the source of truth. Bypass with SPECSMITH_CONVENTIONS=0.

_schema.name: stable identifier used in violation reports
_schema.filesGlob: comma-separated globs; ** matches across path segments, * does not
_schema.excludeGlob: (optional) comma-separated globs of files to skip even if filesGlob matches
_schema.forbiddenPattern: (optional if maxLines is set) JS regex source string; matched against file content
_schema.maxLines: (optional if forbiddenPattern is set) integer; file is in violation when its line count exceeds this number — one violation per oversized file
_schema.skipIfMissing: (optional) glob; if no file in repo matches, the rule no-ops (use to scope rules to projects with specific tooling)
_schema.message: shown to the developer / evaluator when the rule fires

rules.name: no-inline-styles
rules.message: Inline styles are forbidden when Tailwind is configured. Use Tailwind utility classes; for dynamic values, use CSS variables in className (e.g. `className="[--accent:var(--color-ochre)]"`). If a one-off truly needs runtime computation, justify it in a code comment.

rules.name: svg-extract
rules.message: Extract this SVG into its own file under src/components/icons/<IconName>.tsx and import the component. Inline SVGs over 200 characters bloat the parent component and prevent reuse. Constitution: Component Separation.

rules.name: data-access-pattern
rules.message: Direct ORM/database calls are forbidden outside src/lib/<feature>/repository.ts (or src/db/). This applies to React components (server or client), route handlers, server actions, jobs — all of them. Wrap the query in a repository function so callers depend on the domain interface, not the ORM. If your project uses a different data-access location, edit the excludeGlob in .claude/conventions.json.

rules.name: i18n-strings
rules.message: User-facing strings should come from i18n message files, not be inlined in JSX. Replace with `{t('namespace.key')}` (or the project's i18n hook) and add the string to your messages file. The pattern catches multi-word phrases starting with a capital — single-word labels like 'Save' are NOT caught (and probably shouldn't be inlined either). If a string is genuinely not user-facing (e.g. a test ID, a CSS class, a programmatic identifier), refactor so the regex doesn't match (e.g. lowercase, or an attribute the rule doesn't scan). If your project doesn't use i18n at all, remove this rule from .claude/conventions.json.

rules.name: component-size-cap
rules.message: Component file exceeds 300 lines. Files this large almost always mix concerns — view markup, state, data fetching, derivations, validation. Extract: data fetching to a hook (src/hooks/use<X>.ts), derivations to a selector (src/lib/<feature>/selectors.ts), validation to a schema file, sub-views to child components. The cap is a proxy for 'this component is doing too much' — the regex can't tell which kind of logic to extract; you decide. Tune `maxLines` in .claude/conventions.json if 300 is too tight or too loose for your project.

pixelDiff._doc: Visual regression for design fidelity. Run by the evaluator agent via .claude/scripts/pixel-diff.mjs: for each designs/<slug>.html prototype, opens both the prototype and the matching route on the dev server in headless Chromium at the same viewport, diffs the screenshots with pixelmatch, and reports the worst-offending regions as coordinates + intensity. Replaces the evaluator's manual landmark snapshot comparison when it runs. Requires the host project to install playwright + pixelmatch + pngjs (the script skips gracefully if any are missing, and the evaluator falls back to the manual comparison). Disable by setting enabled: false.
pixelDiff._maxDiffPctNote: 5.0 is the v0.10.0 default. A real Next.js / Remix app vs a static-HTML prototype has ~3-6% irreducible drift from font hinting, computed greetings, freshness timestamps, and live data — chasing below 2% rarely converges and burns cycles. Lower this for goldenscreenshot-strict regression on stable views; raise it (or add `masks`) when the visible drift is acceptable.
pixelDiff._storageStateNote: Path to a Playwright storageState JSON (cookies + localStorage), applied ONLY to the actual-side screenshot — never to the reference (designs/*.html doesn't need auth). Use when routes are auth-protected: a project-local wrapper script logs in once, writes the storageState to a known path (e.g. pipeline/storage-state.json), and either sets this field or passes `--storage-state <path>` at the CLI (CLI wins). If the path is set but the file doesn't exist when pixel-diff runs, the script skips with a clear reason instead of silently screenshotting the login page.
pixelDiff._routesNote: If null, the script pairs each designs/<slug>.html with route /<slug> (and index.html with /). Override with an array like [{"design": "designs/dashboard.html", "route": "/dashboard"}] when filename ≠ route, or when only a subset of prototypes are ready to diff.
